content.problemStatement:
You are given a multi-set **S** of **N** integers, and an integer **K**. You want to find the maximum value of Minimum Excluded Value (**MEX**) of the multi-set given that you are allowed to add at most any **K** integers to the multi-set. Find the maximum value of MEX that you can obtain. 

MEX (Minimum Excluded Value) is defined as the smallest non-negative integer (0, 1, 2, ...) that does not appear in a given multiset or array. Few examples of MEX of a multi-set are as follows. MEX of the multi-set {0} is 1, {1} is 0, {0, 1, 3} is 2, {0, 1, 2, 3, 5, 6} is 4.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T denoting the number of testcases. The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and K denoting the size of the multi-set and the maximum number of extra integers that you can add in the multi-set respectively. The second line contains N space-separated integers denoting the multi-set S: S1, S2, .... SN.
content.outputFormat: For each testcase, output the answer in a single line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1. As K = 0, so we can't add any element to the multi-set. Elements of the set are {1, 0, 2}. The MEX value of this set is 3.
Example case 2. As K = 1, you are allowed to add at most 1 element to the multi-set. The multi-set are {1, 0, 2}. You can add element 3 to the multi-set, and it becomes {1, 0, 2, 3}. The MEX value of this multi-set is 4. There is no other way to have higher value of MEX of the set by adding at most one element to the multi-set.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we first need to understand the concept of Minimum Excluded Value (MEX). MEX is the smallest non-negative integer that does not appear in a given multiset or array. We will use a greedy approach to find the maximum value of MEX that can be obtained by adding at most K integers to the multiset. We will sort the multiset and then try to fill the gaps in the sorted multiset by adding integers from 0 to K. The maximum value of MEX will be the length of the longest prefix of the sorted multiset that contains all integers from 0 to the length of the prefix minus one.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N log N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the size of the multiset. The reason is that we are sorting the multiset for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — where N is the size of the multiset. The reason is that we are storing the sorted multiset.
